message:
docs: add end-to-end analysis of BrennerBot with bio-inspired nanochat

Comprehensive test of the complete Brenner Protocol infrastructure with
the bio_inspired_nanochat project and starter pack.

Key findings:
- All core components work: session start, evidence packs, experiments,
  corpus search, excerpt building, anomaly/critique systems
- Fixed ntm cass integration by rebuilding ntm binary from source
- Documented working workflow for structured research sessions

Components tested:
- brenner.ts CLI (session, evidence, experiment, corpus, excerpt commands)
- Agent Mail MCP integration (macro_start_session, summarize_thread)
- ntm integration (cockpit start with role mapping)

Also includes:
- Sample kickoff message excerpt showing research question, hypotheses, tests
- Instructions for viewing agent conversations via brenner.ts mail, ntm, and MCP
- Thread summary showing message flow

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.10","title":"EPIC: Seed Research Programs (Brenner Loop on real codebases)","description":"# Goal
Use the Brenner Bot lab to do actual “computational science” — where experiments are runnable on the computer — so the agents can propose tests, run them, and interpret results together.

This epic is about *using* the lab, not just building it.

---

# Why this matters
A protocol that can’t be used in anger will drift into theater.

Running real sessions:
- validates the artifact schema + linter
- reveals missing primitives
- generates durable examples for onboarding
- produces real progress on your research projects

---

# Initial targets (from the north-star note)
- Dicklesworthstone/model_guided_research
- Dicklesworthstone/bio_inspired_nanochat

---

# Outputs
- 2–3 pilot sessions per project with canonical artifacts committed (where appropriate).
- A short meta-review: what worked, what failed, what to change in the protocol.

---

# Definition of done
- We have at least one “golden session” artifact per target project that demonstrates the Brenner Loop end-to-end.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2.3","title":"Task: Run an experiment-backed round using capture→encode→compile","description":"# Goal
Validate “Science Mode” end-to-end on a real target by running at least one discriminative test and feeding the result back into the session artifact.

This is the proof that the lab is not just producing artifacts, but can actually *update on reality*.

---

# Success criteria (minimum)
- One thread with:
  - role-separated kickoff (or explicitly documented roster)
  - at least one discriminative test T# in the artifact
  - a real command executed by the operator
  - captured `ExperimentResult` JSON file
  - a DELTA message produced by `experiment encode` (or `experiment post`)
  - a compiled artifact updated to reflect the run metadata

---

# Notes / guardrails
- Keep the experiment harmless and reproducible.
- Prefer a test that can be run on a laptop in <5 minutes.
- If results are ambiguous, quarantine them as such; don’t force a narrative.

---

# Output
- Link the thread id
- List the exact commands run
- Commit/persist the updated artifact in the target workspace (if appropriate)
","notes":"Completed pilot thread RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq (project_key=/data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat).

Key commands:
- ./brenner.ts session start --project-key /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat --sender BrownSnow --to BrownSnow,GreenDog --thread-id RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq --role-map 'BrownSnow=hypothesis_generator,GreenDog=test_designer' --excerpt-file /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat/artifacts/RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq/excerpt.md --question '...' --context '...'
- ./brenner.ts mail send ... delta_add_test_T1.md (adds T1)
- ./brenner.ts experiment run --thread-id RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq --test-id T1 --cwd /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat -- uv run python artifacts/RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq/presyn_edge_dependence.py
- ./brenner.ts experiment post --project-key /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat --result-file artifacts/RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq/experiments/T1/20251231T092732Z_f00fc4f9-ff6a-406b-9941-36952bd39607.json --sender BrownSnow --to BrownSnow,GreenDog
- ./brenner.ts mail send ... delta_fill_minimum_artifact.md (fills RT/H/P/A/C, adds T2, adds T1 score)
- ./brenner.ts session write --project-key /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat --thread-id RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq --out-file /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat/artifacts/RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq/artifact.md

Outputs:
- ExperimentResult JSON: /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat/artifacts/RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq/experiments/T1/20251231T092732Z_f00fc4f9-ff6a-406b-9941-36952bd39607.json
- Compiled artifact: /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat/artifacts/RS-20251231-bionanochat-rrp-vs-freq/artifact.md (v1, linter VALID)
- Micro-sim metric: mean_abs_diff_uniform_vs_random=0.1884, random_repeat=0.0 (suggests edge dependence in presynaptic forward() under this synthetic setup).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownSnow","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:44:36.165416593-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T04:34:33.036970098-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T04:34:33.036970098-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:44:36.194710223-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:00.099901123-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:05.171523661-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1es5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:10.242472531-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.10.2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:15.314562077-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -241,6 +241,109 @@ The following workflow successfully runs a complete Brenner Protocol session:
 
 ---
 
+## Viewing Agent Conversations
+
+### Using Agent Mail CLI (brenner.ts)
+
+View thread summary and messages:
+```bash
+# View thread summary with participants and key points
+./brenner.ts mail thread --project-key /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat \
+  --thread-id RS-20260102-bio-nanochat-rrp
+
+# Check inbox for a specific agent
+./brenner.ts mail inbox --project-key /data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat \
+  --agent BlueLake --threads
+```
+
+### Using ntm (Named Tmux Manager)
+
+If agents are running in tmux panes, you can view and interact with them:
+```bash
+# List active sessions
+ntm list
+
+# View pane output for a session
+ntm --robot-tail=RS-20260102-bio-nanochat-rrp --lines=50
+
+# Send a message to all agent panes
+ntm send RS-20260102-bio-nanochat-rrp --all "Please respond to the kickoff message"
+
+# Get session state in JSON format
+ntm --robot-status
+```
+
+### Using Agent Mail MCP Tools Directly
+
+From within Claude Code or another MCP-enabled client:
+```
+# Get thread summary
+mcp__mcp-agent-mail__summarize_thread(project_key, thread_id)
+
+# Fetch inbox for an agent
+mcp__mcp-agent-mail__fetch_inbox(project_key, agent_name)
+
+# Search messages
+mcp__mcp-agent-mail__search_messages(project_key, query)
+```
+
+---
+
+## Sample Kickoff Message Sent
+
+The following kickoff prompt was sent to agents BlueLake and PurpleMountain:
+
+```markdown
+# Kickoff Pack: Bio-Inspired Nanochat (Round 0)
+
+Bead: `brenner_bot-5so.10.2.1`
+Target repo: `/data/projects/bio_inspired_nanochat`
+
+## Research Question (Discriminative)
+
+In Bio-Inspired Nanochat, is **presynaptic vesicle depletion** (RRP clamping)
+functionally distinguishable from an ordinary **frequency penalty / logit bias**?
+If yes, what minimal experiments separate the two?
+
+### Working hypotheses (include third alternative)
+
+- **H1 (Equivalence):** RRP clamping is effectively a tuned frequency penalty;
+  any apparent gains are regularization/cost tradeoffs.
+- **H2 (Mechanistic):** RRP clamping creates *context-/edge-dependent fatigue*
+  that changes attention dynamics in ways a token-count penalty can't reproduce.
+- **H3 (Misspecification):** any "wins" are artifacts (metric confound,
+  sampling/seed effects, compute/capacity mismatch, or evaluation leakage).
+
+## Ranked Discriminative Tests (Cheap → Expensive)
+
+1. **Matched-baseline equivalence test:** Replace RRP clamping with an explicit
+   frequency penalty tuned to match *repetition rate*
+2. **Context-sensitivity test:** Construct prompts with similar token-frequency
+   but different attention structure
+3. **Ablation matrix:** vanilla vs presynaptic-only vs vanilla+freq_penalty
